title: satureja graeca var. canescens
intro: Satureja graeca var. canescens, also known as Greek mountain savory, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Greece. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Greek mountain savory are attributed to its essential oils, including carvacrol and thymol, which have been shown to have analgesic and antiseptic effects, promoting overall health and well-being. Greek mountain savory can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is also used in culinary applications to add flavor to various dishes.
